name: Falling Chocolate Cake with Raspberry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Butter 10 ramekins (about 12 ounces each).
3. Melt chocolate and butter in a double boiler, stirring until mixture is cool.
4. Beat sugar and eggs with an electric mixer until well combined.
5. Add flour and beat until thick and creamy, about 3 minutes.
6. Stir in chocolate-butter mixture until well combined.
7. Divide batter among the ramekins.
8. Place ramekins on a baking sheet and bake in the middle of the oven until cake surfaces are cracked, about 15 minutes (centers will tremble slightly).
9. Cool cakes in ramekins on a rack for 5 to 10 minutes, or until cool enough to handle.
10. Run a thin knife around edges of ramekins and invert warm cakes onto dessert plates.
11. Sprinkle cakes with confectioner's sugar and garnish with mint leaves.
12. Serve cakes with vanilla ice cream and raspberry sauce.
Note: ULB (my brother, the Culinary Institute of America graduate) suggested using pastry flour, cake flour, walnut flour, or a mixture (e.g., 3/4 cake flour, 1/4 nut flour) in place of regular flour.
